_comment: SYNTHETIC sample only — fake names/values to show the payload shape for render.mjs. Do NOT paste real customer/MCP data into this file. Build each real report's payload fresh and pipe it via stdin (see SKILL.md).

narrative.criticalAlert1: Headcount has contracted sharply — down 34.1% year-over-year — with turnover concentrated in a handful of roles. The Developer function carries a critical net turnover gap of -9, the largest in the organization.
narrative.criticalAlert2: Productivity is not the core problem: the average Prodoscore of 54.8 sits above the global benchmark of 48.19. Retention risk here is structural and role-specific rather than a company-wide engagement decline.
narrative.highRiskSubtitle: Top 10 employees by churn probability — prioritize outreach here.
execActions: Open retention conversations with the 5 employees above 20% churn risk this week, starting with the two highest-probability names.
Investigate the Developer function, which shed 9 people with no backfill (net gap -9).
Pair each flagged employee with a manager check-in and a documented retention plan within two weeks.
roleActions: Launch an exit-interview review for the Developer role (-9 gap, 9 departures) to isolate root causes.
Close the QA (-4), Development Operations (-3) and Executive (-2) gaps before they compound.
Protect Engineering — the only role with net positive growth (+2) — and replicate what is working there.
narrative.page4Subtitle: Spread across Sales, Support & Analyst roles
narrative.criticalConcern: This is not a classic disengagement pattern. The high-risk segment is actually more active than peers — longer active time and markedly earlier start times — while headline Prodoscore stays near the domain average. That points to possible overload or burnout rather than checked-out behavior; pair workload review with retention outreach.
